header: S. S. da Grama (SP) / Pocos de Caldas (MG)

lesson.menu_desc: Progress Check -- Review of all structures -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- Progress Check -- The Story So Far
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Tuca Dias | Aula 10 -- The Story So Far

listenings.text: Hi Tuca, it is Marco. I am sending you this message because I want to say how proud I am of you. Think about your journey. Ten lessons ago, you were nervous at the airport, asking simple questions about your gate and your luggage. Then you talked about your life experiences, you learned to ask for directions in a busy city, you ordered food in a restaurant, and you made small talk with strangers. You explored culture, you planned a trip to Mexico City, and finally you prepared with me for the Mont Blanc trek. That is a lot of progress in a short time. You make fewer mistakes now, and you speak with more confidence every week. I really believe you will become fluent if you keep going. This lesson is an important milestone. Look back at how far you have come, and feel proud. The best part of your story is still ahead.

Hey, it is Tuca again. I cannot believe I have reached lesson ten. When I started, I was so nervous. I made a lot of mistakes and I was afraid to speak. But little by little, I improved. Now I feel much more confident. I have learned hundreds of new words, and I can talk about my coffee farm, my travels, and my future plans. My biggest challenge is still speaking on the phone, but I am working on it. Today I am going to re-record my introduction and compare it with my first lesson. I think the difference is going to surprise me. My main goal is to become fluent and travel the world in English. I am proud of my progress, and I am not going to stop now. The story so far is just the beginning.

extra_audio.text: Tuca looked back at her English journey. First, she learned to travel through the airport with confidence. Then she practiced talking about her life experiences. After that, she found her way around a busy city and ordered food like a local. Next, she made small talk and explored new cultures on her trips. Finally, she reached lesson ten, a big milestone, and felt proud of her progress.
